message:
fix(ffs-repair): tighten ISO-8601 parser + reject mid-stream empty repair blocks

Two unrelated correctness fixes in crates/ffs-repair, surfaced
together because they tighten input validation on adjacent code
paths (ownership-record timestamp parsing and repair-symbol-block
layout reading).

ownership.rs — ISO-8601 timestamp parser hardening:

The old parser took "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ", checked length >= 20
and ends_with('Z'), then used &s[a..b].parse() on each digit run.
That accepted nonsense like "0000-00-00T99:99:99Z" because parse()
on "00", "99", etc. happily returns numerics that the downstream
ymd_to_days / Duration::from_secs path then treated as valid time
math. is_expired() callers therefore failed-open on a junk
claimed_at field.

Replaced with byte-level parsing that:
- pins length to exactly 20 bytes (not >=)
- pins each separator position (b'-', b'T', b':', b'Z')
- validates each digit run via a new parse_ascii_digits() helper
  that rejects anything outside b'0'..=b'9'
- enforces calendar validity:
    year >= 1970,
    month in 1..=12,
    day in 1..=days_in_month(year, month),
    hour <= 23, minute <= 59, second <= 59
  via new days_in_month() and is_leap_year() (Gregorian rule:
  divisible by 4, not by 100, unless also by 400)

Added test:
- coordination_record_invalid_calendar_timestamp_treated_as_expired
  pins that claimed_at = "0000-01-01T00:00:00Z" — year-zero,
  rejected by the new validator — falls through to is_expired() ==
  true, so a stale or attacker-crafted record cannot keep an
  ownership lease alive past its TTL by feeding a bogus claim time.

storage.rs — repair-symbol-block layout reading:

read_repair_symbols() walks repair_block_count blocks and accumulates
per-block symbol payloads. The pre-fix loop would silently accept a
layout where some blocks in the middle were empty and later blocks
carried symbols — that violates the layout invariant (empties are
the *tail*, never gaps mid-stream) and would corrupt the recovered
ESI sequence on a partial-write scenario.

Added empty_tail_started flag: once a block returns no symbols, any
subsequent non-empty block trips a hard error
("non-empty repair symbols after empty tail at block N"). Caller
sees a clear RepairFailed instead of a malformed reconstruction.

Also collapsed an

  if let Some(expected) = next_expected_esi {
      if header.symbol_count > 0 && header.first_esi != expected {

into a single Rust-2024 let-chain (`if let ... && header.first_esi
!= expected`), removing the redundant `header.symbol_count > 0`
guard — the parsing path above already guarantees `header.first_esi`
is meaningful when it lands here.

Added test:
- storage_rejects_non_empty_symbol_block_after_empty_tail
  builds a synthetic RepairGroupLayout, writes generation 1 symbol
  blocks, then patches the first block's RepairBlockHeader to
  declare zero symbols (a "bogus empty prefix"), and verifies that
  read_repair_symbols() returns RepairFailed with the empty-tail
  diagnostic instead of silently producing a broken ESI sequence.
- The existing corrupted-symbol-header test was rewritten as a
  let-chain expression to match the surrounding style.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
